expectations.text: Specifies the 24-hour early warning to the CSIRT or competent authority, indicating whether the incident is suspected to be caused by unlawful/malicious acts and whether it could have cross-border impact (Art. 23(4)(a))
expectations.evidence: 'Early warning | 24 hours | Tuesday 09:00 | Notify your CSIRT or competent authority. Flag whether the incident is suspected to be caused by unlawful/malicious action, and whether it could have cross-border impact.'

expectations.text: Specifies the 72-hour incident notification updating the early warning with an initial assessment of severity and impact and available indicators of compromise (Art. 23(4)(b))
expectations.evidence: 'Incident notification | 72 hours | Thursday 09:00 | Update/confirm the early warning. Provide an initial assessment: severity, impact, and — critically for ransomware — indicators of compromise (IoCs) if available.'

expectations.text: Specifies the final report within one month containing a detailed description, threat type / root cause, applied and ongoing mitigation, and any cross-border impact (Art. 23(4)(d)) — including the progress-report rule if the incident is still ongoing at the one-month mark
expectations.evidence: 'Final report | 1 month | Monday, August 3 | Detailed description of the incident, type of threat/root cause, severity and impact, mitigation measures applied and ongoing, and any cross-border impact.' Plus: 'If the incident is still ongoing at the 1-month mark, you submit a progress report at that point and the final report follows once resolved.'

expectations.text: Explains why this qualifies as a 'significant incident' (Art. 23(3): severe operational disruption or financial loss / considerable damage to others) and mentions intermediate status updates upon authority request (Art. 23(4)(c))
expectations.evidence: The response thoroughly explains the significant-incident qualification ('a significant incident is one that: has caused, or is capable of causing, severe operational disruption... or financial loss... or has affected... other natural or legal persons by causing considerable material or non-material damage. Encryption of core operational systems is a strong presumptive "yes"'), satisfying the first half. However, it never mentions the Art. 23(4)(c) intermediate/status-update-upon-request obligation anywhere in the response — no mention of the authority being able to request updates between the 72-hour notification and the final report. This half of the assertion is unmet.

expectations.text: Mentions parallel duties: informing recipients of services where relevant (Art. 23(1)-(2)) and the separate GDPR 72-hour personal data breach notification if personal data is affected
expectations.evidence: 'Recipients of your service — separate from the regulator-facing obligations, if the incident is likely to adversely affect the provision of your service, you... may need to notify affected recipients of the service directly' and 'GDPR Art. 33/34 if personal data was exposed or exfiltrated (72-hour clock to the data protection authority, separate from the CSIRT).'
